ogTitle: Port Augusta West vs Port Bonython: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Port Augusta West and Port Bonython.
heading: Port Augusta West vs Port Bonython — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Port Augusta West, South Australia and Port Bonython, South Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Port Bonython has a lower overall climate risk score (34/100) compared to Port Augusta West (39/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Port Augusta West or Port Bonython safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Port Augusta West has a flood risk score of 43/100, while Port Bonython scores 26/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Port Augusta West scores 52/100 for bushfire risk. Port Bonython scores 47/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Port Augusta West or Port Bonython?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Port Bonython has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.37% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
